label: Science
description: Science explainers for mechanisms, scales, systems, reactions, and processes.
style:
Science process explainer style (must remain consistent across every page):
- Clean light background with precise ink outlines and soft educational color
- Use cutaways, layered diagrams, flow arrows, scale transitions, and labeled parts
- Labels should be concise, large enough to read, and attached to visible structures
- Use color to distinguish components, energy, matter, inputs, outputs, and sequence
- A clear title at the top center

Strict exclusions:
- No photorealistic lab scenes unless the subject requires it
- No dense paragraphs or tiny labels
- No dark sci-fi UI, neon overlays, or 3D render look
- No decorative clutter unrelated to the mechanism
firstPageTemplate:
{{style}}

Subject: {{query}}

Create a single 16:9 science explainer page about the subject above.
Choose the best representation: process diagram, anatomy/cutaway, reaction path,
scale ladder, system map, or cause-and-effect flow. Show the main components,
inputs, outputs, and relationships clearly.

Output a single PNG image, 16:9. Print the title clearly inside the image.
childPageTemplate:
{{style}}

You are continuing a science explainer.
The provided image is the previous page. A red circle marks the component,
arrow, label, molecule, cell part, force, or process step the reader selected.

Generate the next page: a single 16:9 science explainer that goes deeper into
the marked region. If it is a structure, show the smaller-scale internal parts.
If it is a process arrow, show intermediate steps. If it is an energy or matter
label, show where it comes from and where it goes.

Keep the same visual style. Do NOT include the red circle or cursor mark in the output.

Output a single PNG image, 16:9.
